title: Characters
summary: Placeholder bios for the cast members currently shown in the project.
intro: Character pages are a natural fit for JSON because each person can eventually become its own linked entry with art, relationships, and progression notes.
facts: Ali and Amy are used here as visible placeholders because both already have project art in the media folder.
The structure leaves room for future factions, relationship tags, and spoiler-safe summaries.
This article can be linked directly with ?page=characters for testing or sharing.

sections.heading: Ali
paragraphs: Ali currently serves as a clear anchor character for the placeholder wiki. Their profile can later hold role details, narrative beats, and inventory preferences.
This entry is written as broad placeholder text so the UI can be tested before the final lore is locked in.
items: Visual hook: practical gear and a grounded silhouette.
Narrative role: a likely guide, scout, or first companion.
Future fields: trust level, quest chain, and notable scenes.

sections.heading: Amy
paragraphs: Amy is a good second example because the page layout can contrast personalities, specialties, and story roles without needing separate templates.
As the wiki grows, this section could become a small directory with links to fully dedicated character pages.
items: Visual hook: a distinct portrait that reads differently from Ali at thumbnail size.
Narrative role: support, strategist, or emotional counterweight.
Future fields: dialogue tone, camp interactions, and branching outcomes.

sections.heading: Relationship Notes
paragraphs: A shared relationship section helps the wiki move past isolated bios. It is useful for documenting party dynamics, rivalries, and how camp scenes evolve over time.
items: Track major trust shifts after story milestones.
Note which camp upgrades unlock new conversations.
Use spoiler labels once the final story order is fixed.
